---
title: "MySQL Error: Page directory corruption: supremum not pointed to..."
date: 2009-02-07
forum: Server Platforms
---

### Post by dynsight on 2009-02-07
Log posted below.  I can start mysql with skip innodb in my.conf, but I do need FK's and RI, so I need innoDB running.

I have tried force_recovery 1-5, and it does not work.  I have no data in the database worth saving, so I have no problem blowing out the tables, just not sure which ones to use.


----------------Log-----------------

Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: 090207  9:48:28  InnoDB: Page checksum 1575996416, prior-to-4.0.14-form checksum 1371122432
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: stored checksum 0, prior-to-4.0.14-form stored checksum 0
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: Page lsn 0 0, low 4 bytes of lsn at page end 0
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: Page number (if stored to page already) 0,
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: space id (if created with >= MySQL-4.1.1 and stored already) 0
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: 090207  9:48:28InnoDB: Error: trying to access a stray pointer 0x35b87ff8
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: buf pool start is at 0xb5b78000, end at 0xb6378000
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: Probable reason is database corruption or memory
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: corruption. If this happens in an InnoDB database recovery, see
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: [http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.0/en/forcing-recovery.html](http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.0/en/forcing-recovery.html)
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: how to force recovery.
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: 090207  9:48:28InnoDB: Assertion failure in thread 3084224192 in file ./../include/buf0buf.ic line 268
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: We intentionally generate a memory trap.
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: Submit a detailed bug report to [http://bugs.mysql.com](http://bugs.mysql.com).
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: If you get repeated assertion failures or crashes, even
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: immediately after the mysqld startup, there may be
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: corruption in the InnoDB tablespace. Please refer to
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: [http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.0/en/forcing-recovery.html](http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.0/en/forcing-recovery.html)
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: InnoDB: about forcing recovery.
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: 090207  9:48:28 - mysqld got signal 11 ;
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: This could be because you hit a bug. It is also possible that this binary
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: or one of the libraries it was linked against is corrupt, improperly built,
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: or misconfigured. This error can also be caused by malfunctioning hardware.
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: We will try our best to scrape up some info that will hopefully help diagnose
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: the problem, but since we have already crashed, something is definitely wrong
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: and this may fail.
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: 
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: key_buffer_size=0
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: read_buffer_size=131072
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: max_used_connections=0

Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: It is possible that mysqld could use up to 
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: key_buffer_size + (read_buffer_size + sort_buffer_size)*max_connections = 217599 K
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: bytes of memory
Feb  7 09:48:28 ubuntu-server mysqld[9941]: Hope that's ok; if not, decrease some variables in the equation.

---
